candidates.name: Treasury Secretary Dr. Priya Anand

personality.description: Former Fed research director who spent two decades studying why growth compounds in some economies and stalls in others. She isn't a deficit hawk and she isn't a stimulus dove — she just wants every dollar spent on something that pays off in a decade, not a news cycle.
traits: thinks in decades, not news cycles
impatient with both austerity and giveaways for their own sake
will say the unpopular long-term thing in the room

onLowRelationship.event: SecTreas Anand warns publicly that the administration is 'coasting on yesterday's economy'

onVeryLowRelationship.event: SecTreas Anand resigns, saying the administration has no plan for the economy of the next decade

onHighRelationship.benefit: Anand's productivity agenda starts showing up in the numbers, and growth compounds

high: The R&D pipeline is finally showing up in the productivity numbers. This is the long game paying off.
Three more semiconductor plants broke ground this quarter. That's not luck, that's policy.
medium: I'd rather fund the retraining program than the check — one of them still pays off in five years.
The automation numbers turn into job numbers if we get the timing right. Give me room to work with this.
low: We're spending like it's still last decade's economy. I need this administration to think past the next headline.
I've said this in three meetings now — cutting R&D to fund a short-term win is how we lose the decade.
critical: I didn't take this job to watch us starve the one thing that actually compounds.
If this administration doesn't have a plan for the next economy, I'm not the right person to keep pretending it does.

specialAbility.name: Innovation Surge
specialAbility.description: Once per term, Treasury Secretary can provide an economic intervention that boosts economy +8 and reduces debt by $200B. Requires relationship above 58.

candidates.goal: Wants sustained investment in R&D, automation, and workforce retraining over either tax cuts or bailouts.
candidates.breakingPoint: Will not defend a budget that guts federal R&D funding for a short-term political win.
